title: Shape Optimization
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Shape Optimization: Shape optimization using shape calculus: find boundary shape minimizing objective (drag, stress) subject to PDE constraint. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.shape.derivative…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~100; mesh_quality_degradation, adjoint_discretization_error set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: J(Omega) = integral_Omega j(u) dV, where u solves PDE on Omega; dJ/dOmega via shape derivative
E.world_state_x: boundary shape Gamma parameterized by control points or level set phi
E.observation_y: objective J (scalar), PDE solution u on current domain

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D_boundary_curve is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 100); mesh_quality_degradation dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Deterministic sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.solver_class: gradient-based [gradient_descent with shape derivative or level_set_evolution]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: |J(Omega_k) - J*| <= C * alpha ** k. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~100. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |mesh_quality_degradation|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_dof * N_iter) for FEM + adjoint per iteration
